narrative: FY2025 is Tata’s strongest comparative year on recovery and a materially improved year on disposal. Tata led total waste recovered at 19.44 million MT and ranked 2nd on total waste disposed at 20,100 MT, after reducing disposal sharply from FY2024. Tata also ranked 2nd on physical-output intensity at 0.8 MT/tcs, behind SAIL at 0.75 MT/tcs. Jindal remained the absolute waste-generation and turnover-intensity leader, reflecting smaller scale and stainless/EAF circularity, while JSW led recycling and total disposal. SAIL’s profile was bifurcated: best physical-output intensity and very high other recovery, but weakest total disposal due to a 969,314 MT landfill figure linked to old stock. For Tata Steel management, the key message is that Tata’s recovery system is peer-leading, but further improvement is needed in category-specific regulated wastes, especially battery waste, e-waste, biomedical waste, plastic waste and hazardous waste, where Tata ranked 4th in FY2025.
